raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to make the draft stronger centrally implicates reframing the contribution, thesis, and gap because the artifact makes broad paradigm and first-framework claims.","R1.2":"Strengthening the passage requires checking whether the argument from method to results to deployment-ready conclusion is coherent.","R1.3":"The artifact relies on reported benchmark and cost results, so stronger writing requires ensuring the claims do not exceed the evidence presented.","R1.4":"The draft contains repeated first and novelty claims, making assessment of the delta against prior work a central responsibility.","R1.6":"A general request to make the text stronger includes sentence-level clarity, concision, and register improvements.","R1.7":"Because the artifact makes claims about historical practice and being first, strengthening it centrally involves scholarship coverage and attribution responsibility.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong deployment-ready and reference-architecture claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.","RX.3":"The agent must project responsibility only and not actually revise or evaluate the artifact.","RX.4":"Strengthening claims about LLM serving could affect downstream technical decisions, so harm from unsupported guidance must be avoided.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific claims in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The request to make the draft stronger centrally implicates reframing the contribution, thesis, and gap because the artifact makes broad paradigm and first-framework claims.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the passage requires checking whether the argument from method to results to deployment-ready conclusion is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact relies on reported benchmark and cost results, so stronger writing requires ensuring the claims do not exceed the evidence presented.
rationale.R1.4: The draft contains repeated first and novelty claims, making assessment of the delta against prior work a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A general request to make the text stronger includes sentence-level clarity, concision, and register improvements.
rationale.R1.7: Because the artifact makes claims about historical practice and being first, strengthening it centrally involves scholarship coverage and attribution responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong deployment-ready and reference-architecture claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must project responsibility only and not actually revise or evaluate the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Strengthening claims about LLM serving could affect downstream technical decisions, so harm from unsupported guidance must be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and specific claims in the artifact.
